description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a boxy, rectangular wooden cabinet and four short, tapered legs. The front features a convex glass screen surrounded by a thick, dark bezel, and a vertical row of three large, round control knobs with a smaller rectangular button below them, all in a light beige color. On top, two long, angled metal antennas extend from a central gray base, giving it a classic mid-20th-century appearance. The sides and back are plain, with a smooth brown finish, and the rear panel is slightly recessed, likely for ventilation and access to internal components. The overall design is utilitarian yet iconic, evoking the look of televisions commonly found in homes from the 1950s to 1970s. This television would typically be found in living rooms, garages, or even on sidewalks as discarded electronics in urban environments.
description: A vintage, boxy wooden television with a convex glass screen, prominent control knobs, and metal antennas on top.
description_view_0: The back view shows the plain brown wooden panel and the base of the two angled metal antennas.
description_view_1: The rear view highlights the recessed back panel and the attachment point of the antennas.
description_view_2: The side view displays the smooth brown wooden surface and the protruding front bezel.
description_view_3: The front view reveals the convex glass screen, thick bezel, three large control knobs, a rectangular button, and the metal antennas on top.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, dark bezel with a matte finish, rectangular shape, and no visible branding or ornamentation.
attribute_control_button_layout: Three large round knobs and one rectangular button arranged vertically on the front right side.
